title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Yoga Night at Great Bear Vineyards
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Yoga & Wine at the Vineyard in Davis 
 Second Wednesday of Each Month | 6:00–8:00PM May 13 | 6–8pm 
Back by popular demand, FIT4MOM Davis-Woodland and Great Bear Vineyards are teaming up once again for the ultimate midweek reset—yoga, wine, and vineyard vibes. 
Unroll your mat and enjoy an easy to moderate Vinyasa flow led by the talented FIT4MOM Davis-Woodland instructors. Set to groovy tunes and tailored to your skill level, this class is perfect whether you’re a seasoned yogi or just getting started.
As practice winds down, you’ll be guided through an original inspirational meditation before settling in to enjoy a glass of delicious Great Bear wine.
 Ticket Includes: Yoga Class First Glass of Wine
After class, linger a little longer—take a stroll through the lavender fields, set up a picnic, or relax and socialize with new and old friends.
 A workout and wine are the stuff summer dreams are made of.
 What to Bring:• Yoga mat• Towel• Water• Money/card for an extra glass or bottle of wine• Picnic snacks or dinner items• Light jacket or sweater
 Organizer: FIT4MOM Davis-Woodland
We can’t wait to flow, sip, and unwind with you under the evening sky!

title: Free Yoga Practice
description:
Iama Embodied Community
🌀 **Move with intention. Connect with your body. Celebrate community.**

Join us every **Thursday at 6 PM** for an embodied yoga practice designed to help you release tension, ground yourself, and cultivate presence. This is a trauma-informed, all-levels class, no prior yoga experience needed. Expect a Hatha-Vinyasa flow, breathwork, and a space to listen to your body’s wisdom.

🌿 **Every last Thursday of the month, stay after class for a community potluck dinner!**

📍 **Location**: 727 T St. Sacramento CA 95811
✨ **All our events are free. Donations are welcome.**
📩 **Questions? Reach out at** embody.iama@gmail.com
🔗 **Follow us on Instagram:** [@embody.iama](https://www.instagram.com/embody.iama)

title: Sip Yolo with Yolo County Wines
location: Yolo County Vineyard and Winery Association, 13 winery locations!

title: The Sidekicks and Soul Ona Roll
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Live Music & Food Truck at the Vineyard in Davis 
 Friday, May 15 | 5–8pm
Kick off your weekend with a relaxed evening of great wine, live music, and bold, flavorful bites at the vineyard. 
Join us for a laid-back night featuring live music from local favorites The Sidekicks and delicious Cajun & Creole-inspired comfort food from Soul Ona Roll. Sip your favorite estate-grown wines, take in the vineyard views, and enjoy an easygoing atmosphere with friends old and new.
 Ticket Includes: Glass of Wine Live Music
 Evening Schedule: Food Truck Serving: 5–8pm Live Music Performance: 6–7:30pm
 Music by The SidekicksWe’re thrilled to welcome back Marty Cohen & The Sidekicks—one of Northern California’s premier acoustic vocal groups. Featuring Marty Cohen, Ken Rabiroff, and Jeff Sears, their rich 3-part harmonies and distinctive arrangements create a sound described as “urban folk with a pop twist.”
 Food Truck: Soul Ona RollCome hungry! Soul Ona Roll will be serving up mouthwatering Cajun and Creole-inspired classics, including fan favorites like their famous Voodoo Fries. Please support this wonderful local business—no outside food permitted.
 Great wine, soulful music, and unforgettable flavors… the perfect Friday night pairing.
Grab your tickets and join us at the vineyard!

title: Davis Farmers Market
location: Central Park, 301 C Street, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Year-round at Central Park in downtown Davis, thousands gather each week to shop for what is grown, raised, and made locally at our markets. We are dedicated to bringing farmers right to your table, so we stay open rain or shine. 
Walk rows of stalls lined with an array of fruits and vegetables, including certified organic produce, choice meats, chicken, seafood, wine, local eggs and honey. Choose from a variety of fresh-baked goods, plants, flowers and gifts. Enjoy a picnic from one of our many food stands, made from market ingredients.

title: Pick Your Own Bouquet at Park Winters
location: Park Winters, Park Winters, 27850 County Road 26 Winters, CA 95694
description:
This seasonal farm hospitality experience, offered most Saturdays and Sundays, includes everything you need to create a beautiful farm-grown bouquet. Wander through the flower rows and enjoy a peaceful countryside outing while our team guides you on how to clip and gather your blooms. 
Your freshly picked flowers will be wrapped in a moisture-soaked paper wrap to keep them fresh until they are placed in a vase at home. 
While we will accommodate walk-in guests when availability allows, reservations are recommended. On the booking page, simply select the number of bouquets you would like to create. 
As this experience is guided by nature, flower varieties change throughout the season. If field conditions do not meet expectations, a full refund is available upon request.

title: Davis Art Studio Tour
location: www.davisopenstudios.com
description:
Forty-eight artists from across the region will open their Davis studios for the free self-guided Davis Art Studio Tour where hundreds of guests can immerse themselves in the creative spaces where local artwork is being crafted. The two-day public event will take place May 16-17 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. and will include opportunities to view and purchase a wide range of artwork, from sculpture and painting to print making and photography. For information about participating artists and studio locations, visit www.davisopenstudios.com. 
The Davis Art Studio Tour began more than two decades ago and became a casualty of the pandemic until 2023 when a small group of local artists formed a grassroots effort to bring the tour back to life with 21 artists showing their work. In 2024 and 2025, they doubled the number of artists featured, and this year will showcase 48 artists to hundreds of guests on the tour. For more information, visit www.davisopenstudios.com.

title: Sip Yolo with Yolo County Wines
location: Yolo County Vineyard and Winery Association, 13 winery locations!

title: Donut and Wine Pairing
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Donut & Wine Paired Tasting in Davis 
 Saturday, May 16 | 12–5pm
Donuts + wine? Trust us… it’s a pairing you never knew you needed. 
We’ve done the delicious research, and the results are surprisingly sophisticated, outrageously tasty, and seriously fun. Join us at the vineyard for a playful tasting experience that celebrates unexpected flavor combinations and the joy of discovering your new favorite pairing.
 Ticket Includes: Flight of 4 Wines 4 Whole Donuts Paired to Match
 Suggested Pairings Include: Sparkling Brut or Mimosa + Classic Glazed Sparkling Rosé + Cherry or Red Jelly White Dessert Wine + Lemon, Coconut, or Maple Ruby Port-Style Dessert Wine + Chocolate or Maple
Enjoy a guided tasting flight with pairing recommendations, then mix, match, and experiment to discover your own perfect combinations. At the end, cast your vote for the pairing that surprised you most!
Feeling generous? Share the experience with friends by splitting donut pairings and adding separate wine tastings for additional guests ($20 per person).
 Bring your sweet tooth, your curiosity, and your favorite people for an afternoon filled with laughter, indulgence, and vineyard fun.
 Important Details:• Tickets must be purchased in advance• Ticket sales close Thursday, May 14 at 12:00pm• Limited availability to ensure a relaxed, high-quality experience
 Sweet treats, sparkling wine, and vineyard views? Count us in.

title: SpongeBob the Musical, Youth Edition
location: Davis Musical Theatre Company (DMTC), The Jean Henderson Performing Arts Center, 607 Pena Drive, Davis
description:
SpongeBob the Musical, Youth Edition in Davis – May 2 through May 24, 2026
School Matinees: May 5, 2026; 9:15am and noon 
Underwritten by Dr. Jennifer & Scott Goldman, DDS
  
Plunge into this stunning dynamic stage show! When the citizens of Bikini Bottom discover that a volcano will soon erupt and destroy their humble home, SpongeBob and his friends must come together to save the fate of their undersea world. With lives hanging in the balance and all hope lost, a most unexpected hero rises up. The power of optimism really can save the world! 
Ages 5 and up 
  
Directed by Morgan Bartoe
Musical direction by Montana Monce

title: Mosaics Acoustic Guitar & Singing Group
description:
Davis Art, Music & Outdoor Activities Meetup
**Discover Intercultural Mosaics This Winter 2025–2026!**

Join us for a vibrant and uplifting season of virtual learning and community from **December 20, 2025 to March 28, 2026**—all from the comfort of your home on Zoom!

**Registration is required, and the Zoom link will be provided exclusively to those who sign up.**

**Bi-Weekly Friday Zoom Sessions** *(Every 2nd and 4th Friday)*
• **4:00 PM** – Advanced Korean with Stephen
• **5:00 PM** – Intermediate Conversational Chinese with Sam
• **6:00 PM** – Bible Study & Meditation Group with Stephen
• **7:00 PM** – Beginning Chinese with Jane

**Weekly Saturday Zoom Sessions** (total of 12 sessions)
• **4:00 PM** – Beginning Spanish with Viviana
• **5:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Korean with Stephen
• **6:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Japanese with Norio
• **7:00 PM** – English Learners Group with Linda & Chad

**Important Notes:**

1. **No Zoom sessions** on **January 2–3, 2026** (New Year) and **April 3–4, 2026** (Easter).
2. **Special Gatherings (Tentative):**
**• February 14, 2026:** Valentine’s Day & pre–Lunar New Year celebration in person at Phoenix Hall, 2nd Floor, Davis Community Church.
**• March 14, 2026:** Additional in-person gathering (tentative).

**Return to In-Person Meetings:**
We will resume our **regular in-person gatherings on April 11, 2026** at the **2nd floor rooms of Phoenix Hall, Davis Community Church**.

For more information about Intercultural Mosaics classes and activities, visit **[www.nextg.org](http://www.nextg.org)** or contact **Dr. Stephen Moon** at **916-217-5470** or **catalyst@nextg.org**.

title: Davis Art Studio Tour
location: Pence Gallery, 212 D Street, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Davis Art Studio Tour Preview Exhibit  
Studio Tour: May 16-17, 10 AM – 5 PM (free) 
  
This year’s Davis Art Studio Tour returns, and is open the weekend of May 16-17 for art lovers to enjoy for free! Stroll through 40+ art studios in Davis, learn more about artists’ work, and support our local creatives by buying directly from them.  
  
The Pence will preview one piece per artist in the exhibit, and you can plan your tour through the display! Art in various media is on display, including glass, wood, paintings, photography, ceramics, mixed media, and more.  
  
Learn more about the studio tour: https://www.davisopenstudios.com/ 
  
Image: Cathie James-Robinson, “My Happy Place”

title: Winters Farmers Market
location: Downtown Winters, Main Street, Winters, CA, 95694, United States
description:
Join us Downtown Winters every Sunday for the Winters Farmers Market. 
LOCATION: Downtown Main Street between Railroad & 1st Street
DATE & TIME: Every Sunday 9am-1pm (April 26th-October 25th) 
Enjoy local produce, fresh flowers, food vendors & more! 
Interested in being a vendor? 
Email: Simba at simbasheirloomfarm@gmail.com 
www.wintersfarmersmarket.com

title: Singles Wine and Cheese Pairing Experience
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Sips & Sparks – SINGLES Wine & Cheese Pairing Experience  in Davis
 Sunday, May 17 | 12–3pm
Looking to meet new people in a fun, relaxed setting? Skip the swiping and join us at Great Bear Vineyards for an afternoon of wine, cheese, conversation, and connection. 
Indulge your senses (and maybe hand out your phone number ) during this special Singles Wine & Cheese Pairing Experience, featuring a curated flight of four award-winning wines paired with premium artisan cheeses designed to bring out the best in every bite and sip.
The Dates Not Apps team will be onsite helping spark genuine connections through light facilitation, shared interests, and easy conversation starters—so you can mingle naturally without the pressure.
 Ticket Includes: Flight of 4 Wines Paired Artisan Cheese Tasting
 Whether you’re:• A single looking to meet someone new• A foodie who loves delicious pairings• A wine enthusiast eager to explore flavor combinations• Or simply in the mood for a fun Sunday outing…
…this experience is for you!
 What to Expect: MeetCute facilitators helping guests connect naturally Handcrafted wines paired to perfection Fun insights into wine & cheese harmony Relaxed vineyard atmosphere perfect for mingling
Settle into a shady spot, sip something delicious, and enjoy an afternoon designed to help great conversations flow as easily as the wine. Additional wine and snacks will be available for purchase if you’d like to linger a little longer.
 Dietary Restrictions & PreferencesIf you have dietary restrictions or preferences, please email us at:greatbearvineyards@gmail.com
Please include:• Dietary restrictions/preferences(Gluten Free, Vegan, Vegetarian, Nut Free, Pescatarian, Shellfish Free, etc.)• Preferred pronouns• Preferred age range:21–28 | 28–46 | 47–64 | 64+
 Great wine. Great cheese. Great company. You never know who you’ll meet at the vineyard.

title: Artist Talk & Reception with Linda Clark Johnson
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
Join artist Linda Clark Johnson as she discusses her artistic process in working with cyanotype, including her love of sunlight and her ways of gathering and arranging plants. Linda will also show visitors how to get started with cyanotype through an artist demo, as well as explore her current work in her exhibit, *Sunshine & Shadows*, on view at the Pence Gallery from May 2 - June 21, 2026.

Artist Talk & Reception with Linda Clark Johnson
Sunday, May 17, 1-2:30 PM
Free Admission

title: Artist Talk and Reception with Linda Clark Johnson
location: Pence Gallery, 212 D Street, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Artist Talk & Reception with Linda Clark Johnson in Davis 
Sunday, May 17, 1-2:30 PM 
Free Admission 
Join artist Linda Clark Johnson as she discusses her artistic process in working with cyanotype, including her love of sunlight and her ways of gathering and arranging plants. Linda will also show visitors how to get started with cyanotype through an artist demo, as well as explore her current work in her exhibit, Sunshine & Shadows, on view at the Pence Gallery from May 2 – June 21, 2026.

title: Healing Arts One-Time Workshop
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
Part of the Pence's Healing Arts Program and run by facilitator Amy Vidra, this one-time workshop focuses more broadly on themes related to depression, anxiety, or major life changes. Through the process of art making, participants will have opportunities for discussion, education, and connecting with peers in a safe and supportive space.

REGISTRATION ONLINE IS REQUIRED IN ADVANCE through the Pence Gallery's website, and space is limited to the first 8 participants who register. Participants must be 18 or older, and no art experience is necessary. This program is generously sponsored by Bob & Joanne Andresen.

Healing Arts One-Time Workshop
Monday, May 18, 10-11:45 AM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (all materials included)

**Reminder - an RSVP on Meetup does NOT count as registration. Registration is required in advance through the [Pence’s website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

\*If you register for this session and are unable to attend, please let us know in advance.

Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.
source: Meetup Pence Adult Art Programs, Visit Davis, Visityolo Event

title: Conscious Conversations & Embodied Listening
description:
Iama Embodied Community
This is a spiritual community that honors the body’s wisdom and lived experience. Whether you’re healing from religious trauma, deconstructing beliefs, exploring spirituality, or just want to be with other conscious persons, this space is for you.

Expect conscious conversations, meditation, breathwork, sound healing, and other somatic practices to help reconnect with ourselves and each other.

we share a potluck dinner every last Monday of the month.

📅 **Date & Time**: Mondays 6-8 PM
📍 **Location**: 727 T St. Sacramento, Ca, 95811
💜 **All our events are free, donations supported.**
✨ **No prior experience or spiritual beliefs necessary.**
📩 **RSVP/ Questions? Reach out at** embody.iama@gmail.com
🔗 **Follow us on Instagram:** [@embody.iama](https://www.instagram.com/embody.iama)

PS: we meet in the social hall of Parkview Presbyterian Church. Please find us through the back entrance, the brown gate on Solons alley. You may park in the back, move the orange post to park and please return it when you leave.

title: Gentle yoga
description:
Iama Embodied Community
This is a slow and mindful practice that emphasizes deep breathing, body awareness, and nervous system regulation. This class is accessible to all abilities, providing a supportive and trauma-informed space to reconnect with the body.

Sessions alternate between floor yoga and chair yoga (on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday). Stay a bit longer on the last Tuesday of the month and join us for coffee or tea afterward!

Like all our events, this class is free, donations are welcome.

title: Watercolor Portrait Painting with Misuk Goltz
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
This watercolor portrait workshop with Misuk Goltz will teach you how to mix skin tones and develop a layering process to capture a subject’s likeness and character. Students will explore an expressive style rather than realistic perfection, focusing on facial structure, features, and shadows. Through hands-on demonstrations, you’ll be guided until your portrait comes to life. Portrait painting experience isn’t necessary, but participants should be familiar with the basics of drawing and watercolor painting.

Watercolor Portrait Painting with Misuk Goltz
Saturday, May 23, 10 AM - 2:30 PM
$120 Members, $135 Non-Members (materials not included)

**Registration is required through the [Pence's website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

Questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.
source: Meetup Pence Adult Art Programs, Visit Davis, Visityolo Event

title: Sip Yolo with Yolo County Wines
location: Yolo County Vineyard and Winery Association, 13 winery locations!

title: Park Winters Garden Tour
location: Park Winters, Park Winters, 27850 County Road 26 Winters, CA 95694
description:
Park Winters is surrounded by hundreds of acres of farmland with unobstructed views of the Berryessa Blue Ridge Reserve. Originally planted in 1865, our gardens take you back in time—now lovingly curated, manicured and maintained. 
Join us for a guided tour, take in the beauty of 150 year old trees and stroll through our sunken garden filled with lavender and snowy white roses. The classic cypress trees soar and native songbirds call as we experience the scents and scenes of the western Yolo County countryside. This experience is approximately a 90 minute walking tour, 
*Garden Tours are on the 2nd and 4th Saturday’s of each month, with exception for July – August, when we take a pause due to high temperatures – Garden Tours will resume in September.

With lives hanging in the balance and all hope lost, a most unexpected hero rises up. The power of optimism really can save the world! 
Ages 5 and up 
  
Directed by Morgan Bartoe
Musical direction by Montana Monce

title: Winters Theatre Company Presents Murder on the Nile
location: Winters Opera House Ballroom, 13 Main St, Winters, 95694, United States
description: Get ready to take a trip down the Nile this summer as the Winters Theatre Company presents Agatha Christie’s Murder on the Nile!

title: Pick Your Own Bouquet at Park Winters
location: Park Winters, Park Winters, 27850 County Road 26 Winters, CA 95694
description:
This seasonal farm hospitality experience, offered most Saturdays and Sundays, includes everything you need to create a beautiful farm-grown bouquet. Wander through the flower rows and enjoy a peaceful countryside outing while our team guides you on how to clip and gather your blooms. 
Your freshly picked flowers will be wrapped in a moisture-soaked paper wrap to keep them fresh until they are placed in a vase at home. 
While we will accommodate walk-in guests when availability allows, reservations are recommended. On the booking page, simply select the number of bouquets you would like to create. 
As this experience is guided by nature, flower varieties change throughout the season. If field conditions do not meet expectations, a full refund is available upon request.

title: Conscious Conversations & Embodied Listening
description:
Iama Embodied Community
This is a spiritual community that honors the body’s wisdom and lived experience. Whether you’re healing from religious trauma, deconstructing beliefs, exploring spirituality, or just want to be with other conscious persons, this space is for you.

Expect conscious conversations, meditation, breathwork, sound healing, and other somatic practices to help reconnect with ourselves and each other.

we share a potluck dinner every last Monday of the month.

📅 **Date & Time**: Mondays 6-8 PM
📍 **Location**: 727 T St. Sacramento, Ca, 95811
💜 **All our events are free, donations supported.**
✨ **No prior experience or spiritual beliefs necessary.**
📩 **RSVP/ Questions? Reach out at** embody.iama@gmail.com
🔗 **Follow us on Instagram:** [@embody.iama](https://www.instagram.com/embody.iama)

PS: we meet in the social hall of Parkview Presbyterian Church. Please find us through the back entrance, the brown gate on Solons alley. You may park in the back, move the orange post to park and please return it when you leave.

title: Gentle yoga
description:
Iama Embodied Community
This is a slow and mindful practice that emphasizes deep breathing, body awareness, and nervous system regulation. This class is accessible to all abilities, providing a supportive and trauma-informed space to reconnect with the body.

Sessions alternate between floor yoga and chair yoga (on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday). Stay a bit longer on the last Tuesday of the month and join us for coffee or tea afterward!

Like all our events, this class is free, donations are welcome.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Free Yoga Practice
description:
Iama Embodied Community
🌀 **Move with intention. Connect with your body. Celebrate community.**

Join us every **Thursday at 6 PM** for an embodied yoga practice designed to help you release tension, ground yourself, and cultivate presence. This is a trauma-informed, all-levels class, no prior yoga experience needed. Expect a Hatha-Vinyasa flow, breathwork, and a space to listen to your body’s wisdom.

🌿 **Every last Thursday of the month, stay after class for a community potluck dinner!**

📍 **Location**: 727 T St. Sacramento CA 95811
✨ **All our events are free. Donations are welcome.**
📩 **Questions? Reach out at** embody.iama@gmail.com
🔗 **Follow us on Instagram:** [@embody.iama](https://www.instagram.com/embody.iama)

title: JOURNEY THROUGH THE REDWOODS
description:
Iama Embodied Community
Tucked away in the Oakland Hills, this hidden gem is home to mostly second- and third-generation coastal redwoods, descendants of
the ancient giants that once stood alongside the sacred California grizzly and soaring condors. Today, these trees stretch skyward, carrying that legacy forward.

This will be an easy to moderate 3-mile journey, with approximately 350 feet of cumulative elevation gain. Along the way, we’ll encounter a variety of wild berries, from strawberries and huckleberries to the soft, velvety touch of thimbleberries and more.

Michael will share practical knowledge on
how to locate water in the landscape, and reading subtle signs from birds, trees, and leaves.
You’ll also learn how to recognize when you’re nearing the ocean, guided by the presence of specific plant species.

Elizabeth will offer insights into crows and ravens, marveling at their deep sense of community, reverence, and the way they invite us to
see the world with curiosity and wonder.
We’ll finish the journey with a relaxed tea gathering. Hot water will be provided; please bring your favorite tea and a snack to share.

title: Art in Action: Spectacular Seed Bombs
description:
Pence Family Art Workshops
Learn about the ancient Japanese practice of seed bombs! Using air-dry clay and native wildflower seeds, make seed bombs that you can later plant outdoors or in a garden. Decorate your seed bombs with dried petals and stamps, and design and illustrate a plant care card to help you take care of the wildflowers you plant.

Art in Action: Spectacular Seed Bombs
Saturday, May 30, 1-3 PM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (per person, all materials included)

**How it works:**
Art in Action engages kids in art projects that are fun, creative, and collaborative with their family members. This is a drop-in program for ages 5-12. No registration in advance is required, but space and supplies are limited to the first 25 participants and are available on a first come, first served basis.

When you arrive, pay with cash or card at the front desk. A parent or adult caregiver is required to stay, but only has to pay for the project for themselves if they are also doing art. Stay as long or as short as you like over the two hour period.

Become a Member starting at $70/year and save! If you join the Pence Gallery when you attend this workshop, you can enjoy Member pricing for Art in Action, plus receive a $5 gift certificate to our gift shop as a thank you. Membership supports art for all ages and makes creative moments like this possible—jump in and be part of it.

We have a different Art in Action family workshop every month! Learn more at [https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/](https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/).

This unique monthly program is generously sponsored and made possible by Mary Stephens DeWall and Les DeWall.

title: Experience Argentina from North to South
location: International House Davis, 10 College Park, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Experience Argentine culture through sight, sound, taste, and movement in this interactive, community-centered event. Designed as a participatory journey, the program invites you to connect with traditions, share stories, and build meaningful connections across cultures. 
Part 1: Journey Through Argentina in Davis | 60 minutes 
Begin in the I-House’s lounge with an interactive exhibition highlighting five regions of Argentina. Through traditional crafts, instruments, textiles, and photo displays, you’ll explore the country’s cultural diversity while enjoying small tastings like Malbec, dulce de leche, and chimichurri. Argentine community members will share stories and guide the experience. 
Part 2: Folklore Dance Workshop | 90 minutes 
In the I-House’s Community Room, join a 90-minute dance workshop led by Veronica Flores. Through movement and music, you’ll learn the basics of traditional dances such as carnavalito and gato in a welcoming, beginner-friendly environment. 
Part 3: Peña-Inspired Musical Gathering | 90 minutes 
The evening concludes outdoors with a relaxed, live music gathering inspired by a traditional Argentine peña. Set in a social, communal atmosphere, this experience blends music, conversation, and shared space, inviting you to listen, dance, and connect with others.

title: Botanical Blueprints with Linda Clark Johnson
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
You don’t need to be an artist to enjoy making beautiful blue cyanotypes! In this introductory class with artist Linda Clark Johnson, you’ll learn all the basics of this fun and easy alternative photographic technique. Using light-sensitive chemicals, you’ll expose prints of plants in the sun and process them in water. By the time you leave, you’ll have 4 gorgeous 8 x 10” blueprints and the know-how to create more at home.

Botanical Blueprints with Linda Clark Johnson
Sunday, May 31, 10 AM - 2 PM
$115 Members \| $125 Non\-Members
(All materials included except for flowers and foliage, which participants should bring)

**Registration is required through the [Pence's website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.
source: Meetup Pence Adult Art Programs, Visit Davis

title: Blood on the Clocktower at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
The first Wednesday of every month, we play _Blood on the Clocktower_, a social deduction game in the vein of Werewolf/Mafia/The Resistance, with a few twists!

We'll have a circle of chairs reserved inside Blue Note, near the back, with a Yolo County Board Game Gathering sign on the table. Just say hello, and we'll show you some games, pull some players together, and get to having fun!

We also usually have a few tables of players playing other board games, if social deduction isn't your bag!

Get a feel for Blood on the Clocktower here: https://youtu.be/4sfa8_kNxsQ
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Art in Action: Creative Sun Print Collages
description:
Pence Family Art Workshops
Create beautiful blue sun prints of plants and flowers! Using special blue photo paper, the sun’s rays, and some water, create amazing prints (known as cyanotypes) of leaves, flower petals, and more. Transform these prints into one-of-a-kind collages through cutting, layering, and adding natural materials and other found objects.

Art in Action: Creative Sun Print Collages
Sunday, June 7, 1-3 PM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (per person, all materials included)

**How it works:**
Art in Action engages kids in art projects that are fun, creative, and collaborative with their family members. This is a drop-in program for ages 5-12. No registration in advance is required, but space and supplies are limited to the first 25 participants and are available on a first come, first served basis.

When you arrive, pay with cash or card at the front desk. A parent or adult caregiver is required to stay, but only has to pay for the project for themselves if they are also doing art. Stay as long or as short as you like over the two hour period.

Become a Member starting at $70/year and save! If you join the Pence Gallery when you attend this workshop, you can enjoy Member pricing for Art in Action, plus receive a $5 gift certificate to our gift shop as a thank you. Membership supports art for all ages and makes creative moments like this possible—jump in and be part of it.

We have a different Art in Action family workshop every month! Learn more at [https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/](https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/).

This unique monthly program is generously sponsored and made possible by Mary Stephens DeWall and Les DeWall.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.

title: Beginning Charcoal Drawing
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
Learn the basics of charcoal drawing with Sophie Jorcino! In this one-day workshop, you will learn value shading, texture techniques, and sketching exercises, all while learning to use different charcoal materials that are included with the class fee. This workshop is open to everyone ages 16 and up- no art experience required.

Beginning Charcoal Drawing
Saturday\, June 27 \| 11:30 AM \- 3:30 PM
$75 Members, $95 Non-Members (All materials included)

**Registration is required through the [Pence's website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.

title: Blood on the Clocktower at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
The first Wednesday of every month, we play _Blood on the Clocktower_, a social deduction game in the vein of Werewolf/Mafia/The Resistance, with a few twists!

We'll have a circle of chairs reserved inside Blue Note, near the back, with a Yolo County Board Game Gathering sign on the table. Just say hello, and we'll show you some games, pull some players together, and get to having fun!

We also usually have a few tables of players playing other board games, if social deduction isn't your bag!

Get a feel for Blood on the Clocktower here: https://youtu.be/4sfa8_kNxsQ
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering
